name: Parietal Lobe
location: Superior-posterior region of cerebral cortex behind the frontal lobe
description: Integrates somatosensory inputs including touch, temperature, spatial navigation, coordinate transformation, and numerical processing.
functions: Visuospatial attention and body schema mapping
bloodSupply: Middle Cerebral Artery (MCA) & Anterior Cerebral Artery (ACA)
clinicalImportance: Damage to right parietal lobe leads to contralateral spatial neglect; left damage causes agraphia and acalculia.
researchNotes: Intraparietal sulcus (IPS) activity correlates directly with spatial working memory load.
interestingFacts: Albert Einstein's parietal lobes were 15% wider than average brains.
